description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing configuration, featuring a brown fuselage and wings with visible rigging wires connecting the upper and lower wings. The aircraft has a wooden propeller mounted at the nose, and the engine is exposed with visible exhaust pipes along the sides. The tail section displays a vertical stabilizer with a tricolor roundel and a horizontal stabilizer, both in matching brown with accent markings. The fuselage bears military insignia, including roundels and a code 'T6', suggesting a historical or military context. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, with a single seat visible. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable.
description: A vintage brown biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, exposed engine, and fixed landing gear, featuring military insignia and a wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, wooden propeller, and exposed engine details.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the roundel insignia, 'T6' marking, open cockpit, and tail with tricolor fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the full wingspan with rigging wires.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit, engine, and tail markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and rigging wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; standard rudder and elevator surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine, with no canopy.
